message:
test(fcp-manifest): pin CIDR validation boundary cases (sd1ll)

Add crates/fcp-manifest/tests/cidr_validation_boundaries.rs pinning
the CIDR validation contract on
NetworkConstraints.cidr_deny (lib.rs:2285-2290). Bead title says
fcp-core but the actual code lives in fcp-manifest (the only
workspace crate that depends on `ipnet`); tests are placed where
the code is.

21 tests cover both layers:

IpNet parser (the underlying validator):
- IPv4 /0, /32, full prefix-length sweep [0, 32]
- IPv6 /0, /128, full prefix-length sweep [0, 128]
- Reject IPv4 /33 and /255
- Reject IPv6 /129 and /255
- Reject negative / non-numeric / empty prefix
- Reject bare address without /<n>
- Reject 999.0.0.0/8, 10.0.0/8, 10.0.0.0.0/8, hello/8, gggg::/16,
  /8, ""

End-to-end manifest validation (the user-facing surface):
- Accept a representative valid mix
- Accept empty cidr_deny (the documented default)
- Reject /33, /129, malformed address, non-IP string, bare address
- On a mixed list, surface the FIRST invalid entry's value in the
  error message
- Error mentions the `cidr_deny` field name and the offending CIDR
  string verbatim

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
